course_description:
Independent research and thesis preparation under the guidance of a supervisor.

SOSC6012 Honours Research Thesis refers to a program of full-time study in which the honours research thesis is completed over two consecutive semesters . Students are expected to complete the equivalent of 4 units of thesis work in the first semester and 8 units of thesis work in the second semester.

A thesis topic will be accepted only if Thesis Advisors are available in the general discipline area in which the student wants to study. This will be decided by the Honours Convenor in consultation with other staff members.

An honours degree is an intensive program of study following a three year Bachelor degree. For your honours thesis, you must address and meet the criteria and standards of assessment for the thesis set out in the marking guide available in the 'Assessment' folder on Blackboard. The following are general guidelines:

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Restricted to students enrolled in the BA(Hons), BSocSc(Hons) or BCCJ(Hons)

course_contacts.name: Student Enquiries School of Social Science
course_contacts.notes: Level 3, Michie Building (09), St Lucia campus, The University of Queensland. Monday-Friday, 9:00am-12:00pm, 1:00pm-4:00pm.

course_aims:
The main aims of the Honours year are:

• ﾠﾠ to enable students to pursue further study in a specialised area in more depth than is possible in a 3-year Bachelors degree program;

• ﾠﾠ to enhance students’ capacity for independent research and to acquire research skills appropriate to their areas of specialisation; and

•ﾠﾠﾠ to train to a standard sufficient for enrolment in postgraduate research degrees and for a specialist career in the field of research.

The Honours thesis constitutes a major component of the Honours program.ﾠ Other coursework complements the specific aims of the student's thesis research.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply a range of skills and analyse a body of knowledge, and thus to demonstrate the potential to succeed at more advanced levels
Contribute to the chosen field of study through the development of new ideas or critical review of existing perspectives
Demonstrate an ability to find and assess sources of information, and particularly to estimate the relevance of arguments and methods for tackling specific problems.
Present scholarly arguments, logically and lucidly.

assessment_details.title: Thesis Draft
assessment_details.task_description: A complete draft of the thesis AND accompanying Turnitin report (PDF copy) must be presented for feedback from the Thesis Advisor(s). Please note that if you submit your draft after this date, you may not receive timely feedback. If your thesis is not available in draft form by this date, you should submit whatever written work you have as well as a Turnitin report. This is very important as it will give both you and your Thesis Advisor time to review your progress, address any issues, and plan the completion of your thesis. Failure to submit written drafts by this date will almost certainly mean a poor result for your final product. Please refer to the SOSC6012 Blackboard for detailed guidance on the suggested content and formatting of the thesis document. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submitted directly via email to the student's Thesis Advisor(s).
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
This is a formative assessment only. No extensions are required if you are unable to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date. You should notify your Thesis Advisor that the draft will be late and discuss a schedule for feedback and completion.
Late submission
This is a formative assessment only. Failure to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date will not result in any penalty, but it will impact on receiving timely feedback from your Advisor.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: This is a formative assessment only. Failure to submit your thesis full draft and Turnitin report to your Thesis Advisor by the due date will not result in any penalty, but it will impact on receiving timely feedback from your Advisor.

assessment_details.title: Final Honours Thesis
assessment_details.task_description: Task: Students are to produce a thesis document between 12,000 and 15,000 words in length, which is the product of their own independent research undertaken during the Honours period. The thesis is to be written, structured, and formatted under the guidance of the student's Thesis Advisor(s) and in accordance with the guidelines provided on Blackboard. Word count exclusions : text in figures, diagrams, and tables; fore-pages (title page up to the first page of the first or introductory chapter), appendices, and reference list or bibliography. Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools : This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst there may be instances where it is appropriate for students to use AI tools in their thesis project, successful completion of the thesis assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. AI tools (including generative AI and machine translation tools) should be used with caution as they may lead to disputes about copyright, authorship, plagiarism, and research integrity. If you plan to use AI tools in the research or writing of your thesis, you MUST consult with your Thesis Advisor and/or Honours Convenor to discuss how you plan to use it, whether this usage is appropriate, how to document this usage, and how to acknowledge AI usage in your submitted assessment. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines on how to reference AI in your assessment can be found here: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/ai-tools-assignments (additional guidance will be provided on Blackboard). UQ guidelines on AI usage can be found at the UQ AI Student Hub : https://web.library.uq.edu.au/study-and-learning-support/ai-student-hub including UQ rules for using AI. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
You must submit your final thesis document electronically by the due time, on the due date.
Your final thesis document must be submitted via Turnitin on blackboard. To submit your assignment electronically log in to https://learn.uq.edu.au/ultra with your UQ username and password, then click on Course Code>>Assessment>>Assignments, and use the appropriate assignment submission link for each piece of assessment. No e-mailed submissions of assessments will be accepted.
Turnitin links will be configured to permit early submission of the thesis. Students will have the opportunity to submit draft thesis chapters to a dedicated Turnitin portal prior to submission of the final assignment in order to review similarity index content and to improve academic writing practice in accordance with UQ Academic Integrity policies.
By uploading your assignment via Turnitin, you are certifying that the work you submit is your own work except where correctly attributed to another source. Do not submit your thesis if it contains any work that is not your own. Please note that on the preview page, your thesis will be shown without formatting. Your thesis will retain formatting and your coordinator will be able to see formatted theses. Once you have submitted your thesis you are able to go back and view your submission with the correct formatting.
You are required to retain proof of submission of your thesis. Your Digital Receipt is available for download from your Assignment Dashboard. If you cannot see your submission and download your digital receipt, your thesis has not been successfully submitted, please submit again. If you are unable to submit your thesis by the due date, you need to apply for an extension as set out in Extension section below.
If you are experiencing technical difficulties with Blackboard, please contact the ITS Support Team .
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
You must submit a request for an extension as soon as it becomes clear you need an extension. Your request should be submitted no later than the assessment item's due date and time.
The request must be accompanied by supporting documentation corroborating the reason for the request. A list of acceptable reasons for an extension and the evidence you must provide can be found here . Your request may be refused if you do not meet the acceptable reasons for an extension. The student submitting the request is fully responsible for all supporting documentation that is provided with the request and should ensure all documents are authentic.
In the School of Social Science, extensions on the basis of an approved Student Access Plan (SAP) or an Extension Verification Letter (EVL) can be approved for a maximum period of 7 calendar days. Subsequent extensions for a piece of assessment will require additional supporting documentation (e.g., a medical certificate or other supporting evidence listed on my.UQ ).
Extension requests exceeding the maximum extension period stated for a piece of assessment will only be considered under exceptional circumstances (circumstances outside of your control) with additional supporting documentation.
Late applications must include evidence of the reasons for the late request, detailing why you were unable to apply for an extension by the due date and time. The School of Social Science will not accept personal statements.
Students may be asked to supply the work they have completed to date on the assessment piece. This is to establish what efforts have already been made to complete the assessment, and whether the proposed work plan is feasible. Extension requests are processed and managed by the School of Social Science Administration Team.
Thesis extensions are likely to delay your graduation by up to one semester.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Work will not be accepted if it is more than one week (7 calendar days) late without prior approval.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
